interpretation: Of 20 queryable pearl variants, 85.0% (17/20) are completely absent from gnomAD v4 and 100% are absent or ultra-rare (AF<0.0001). Only 3 promoter pearls show any population frequency (max AF=2.07e-05, AC<=17). For comparison, 73.7% (14/19) of sampled benign HBB variants are also absent from gnomAD, reflecting the extreme conservation of the entire HBB locus. Neither Mann-Whitney (p=0.41) nor Fisher exact test (p=0.45) reached significance, which is expected given the floor effect: HBB is so conserved that even benign variants are mostly absent from population databases. The key finding is that ALL 20 queryable pearl SNVs are either absent or ultra-rare (AF<2.1e-05), consistent with purifying selection against these variants. The 5 missense pearls at positions 5226598, 5226613, 5226643 (exon 1 region) are all completely absent from gnomAD, supporting their classification as functionally impactful.

promoter_pearls.note: 5'UTR/promoter variants - 3 show ultra-rare frequencies consistent with mild purifying selection
